club.title: The club
club.intro: Founded in Tunis in 1920, Club Africain is one of the emblematic institutions of Tunisian sport, known for its red-and-white identity, its competitive culture, and its lasting connection with supporters across generations.
club.historyBody1: From its earliest years, the club grew into far more than a football badge. It became a civic and sporting symbol, carrying ambition on the pitch while also building a wider sporting culture through multiple sections and youth development.
club.historyBody2: That long story still matters today: every squad, from seniors to academy levels, sits inside the same shared identity of discipline, intensity, and public expectation.
club.honourLeague: Domestic league success helped establish Club Africain as a permanent title contender and one of the country's defining sporting names.
club.honourCup: Cup runs and knockout campaigns strengthened the club's image as a side capable of handling pressure, rivalry, and decisive nights.
club.honourContinental: Continental and regional appearances extended that reputation beyond Tunisia and placed the club in wider North African and African competition.
club.honourSections: The club story also includes handball, basketball, volleyball, tennis, and other sections that give the institution a true multi-sport footprint.
club.supportersIdentity: The stadium experience is inseparable from the club's identity: red and white colors, sharp visual culture, and a crowd that expects commitment every time the shirt is worn.
club.supportersCulture: Supporters are not a backdrop. They shape atmosphere, pressure, and memory, turning ordinary fixtures into events with emotional weight.
club.supportersVenue: Whether at a major football ground or an indoor hall for another section, the same expectation follows the team: energy, dignity, and competitive pride.
club.timeline1920: The club is founded in Tunis and begins building the identity that will define its public image for generations.
club.timeline1947: Post-war growth helps solidify Club Africain as a major force in the national sporting landscape.
club.timeline1974: The club enters a stronger era of domestic competitiveness and supporter visibility across several sections.
club.timeline1991: Continental ambition becomes a defining part of the club narrative and of how supporters measure major seasons.
club.timelineToday: Club Africain continues to combine first-team pressure, youth development, and multi-sport ambition under the same badge.
